Figure 1:Conical Singularity Scaling Law:σmax\sigma_{\max}vs.ϵ\epsilonfor Qwen3-0.6B.Log-log plot of the maximum Jacobian singular valueσmax\sigma_{\max}against the topological mollifierϵ\epsilonat 5 representative layers (0, 6, 13, 20, 27).
All 5 curves trace parallel lines with measured slopeα=−0.5000\alpha=-0.5000andR2=1.000000R^{2}=1.000000, in exact agreement with the theoretical predictionσmax=Cℓ⋅ϵ−1/2\sigma_{\max}=C_{\ell}\cdot\epsilon^{-1/2}fromtheorem2.
The grey solid line shows the unweighted theoretical boundd/ϵ\sqrt{d/\epsilon}.
Vertical offsets between layers encode the layer-wise gauge massCℓC_{\ell}, which increases monotonically from shallow to deep layers.

Figure 2:Lie–Trotter Torsion Interferometer: Cosine Similarity atα=1\alpha\!=\!1.Each point representscos⁡(δ,[𝒯,ℛ]Ψ)\cos(\delta,\,[\mathcal{T},\mathcal{R}]_{\Psi})at a single (layer, token position) pair.
Green dashed line: median; grey dashed line: isotropic random baseline1/d1/\sqrt{d}.
Both architectures—Qwen3-0.6B (left) and GPT-2 (right)—exhibit systematic positive alignment far exceeding the random baseline, with overall medians of+0.793+0.793and+0.842+0.842, respectively.

Figure 3:Per-Layer Distribution of Torsion Alignment.Box plots ofcos⁡(δ,[𝒯,ℛ]Ψ)\cos(\delta,\,[\mathcal{T},\mathcal{R}]_{\Psi})across 16 token positions at each sampled layer.
Mid-network layers (Qwen3 L12:0.8790.879, GPT-2 L9:0.9330.933) achieve peak alignment, while final layers (Qwen3 L27:0.1120.112, GPT-2 L11:−0.188-0.188) show degradation consistent with strong higher-order BCH contributions near the unembedding boundary.

Figure 9:Universal Phase Transition under Noise Isolation across Three Architectures.Perplexity (PPL) vs. sequence lengthNNfor uniform random noise haystacks at full spectral capacity (α=1.0\alpha=1.0). All three models—Qwen3-0.6B (dhead=64d_{\mathrm{head}}\!=\!64), LLaMA-3.1-8B (dhead=128d_{\mathrm{head}}\!=\!128), and Gemma-3-1B (dhead=288d_{\mathrm{head}}\!=\!288)—exhibit a universal PPL cliff atN≈32→48N\approx 32\to 48(shaded region), followed by saturation. Error bars show standard deviation over 5 independent random seeds.
